title: Content in unmapped fields in Jira integration are deleted in jira board

posts.author: guillemarqueta (Guillermo Marqueta)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,
by using the integration to add new Monday issues to a Jira board, whenever we update a mapped field inside Monday, it updates the Jira issue. That’s great.

The problem I find is when we have manually added some extra information to the unmapped fields directly in Jira (i.e. Description). When we update one of the fields in Monday (i.e. Status), the automation deletes all data from the unmapped fields.
See here my Jira user is the one executing the automation.

Captura de pantalla 2021-09-09 a las 10.04.331168×758 105 KB

Can anyone help?

Thanks

posts.author: Flowace (Flowace)
posts.cookedHtml:
Guillermo Marqueta:

Hi,
by using the integration to add new Monday issues to a Jira board, whenever we update a mapped field inside Monday, it updates the Jira issue. That’s great.

The problem I find is when we have manually added some extra information to the unmapped fields directly in Jira (i.e. Description). When we update one of the fields in Monday (i.e. Status), the automation deletes all data from the unmapped fields.
See here my Jira user is the one executing the automation.

Captura de pantalla 2021-09-09 a las 10.04.331168×758 105 KB

Can anyone help?

Thanks

It’s great to hear that your integration between Monday and Jira is working well for mapped fields.
However, it seems there’s an issue when you manually add extra information to unmapped fields in Jira, and then when you update a mapped field in Monday, the automation deletes the data from the unmapped fields.
This could be happening because the automation is set to update the entire Jira issue when changes are made in Monday. To avoid this data loss, you may need to configure the automation in a way that it doesn’t overwrite unmapped fields in Jira.
You should review your automation settings and ensure that only the mapped fields get updated, leaving the unmapped fields intact.
As for advice, if you encounter difficulties in adjusting the automation settings, you might consider using a tool like “Flowace” for Jira integration, as it provides more control over these types of integrations and allows you to fine-tune how data is synchronized between the two platforms.
This can help you avoid unintended data loss and ensure a smoother workflow between Monday and Jira.
